strategy.title: AI Strategy & Build-with-You

projects.heading: What Happened When I Turned the AI On
projects.subheading: Real systems running in production for real businesses.
projects.allDescription: Every AI system I've built for real businesses. Production workflows, voice agents, and automation that runs daily.
projects.metaDescription: Real AI automation systems running in production. Case studies from businesses in Finland, Sweden, and globally.

scalling-school-email.title: AI Email Automation
scalling-school-email.problem: Spending hours manually reading, categorizing, and replying to emails. Important messages were getting buried.
scalling-school-email.solution: Built an n8n workflow with AI email classification, knowledge base integration, conversation history tracking, and automated draft generation that sounds human.
results: Human-sounding drafts ready to review and send
Hours of daily email work eliminated

hvac-quote-system.title: Intelligent Quote System
hvac-quote-system.problem: Complex email handling with attachments (images, PDFs, Excel, Word), pricing calculations, and parts catalog lookups taking up one full employee's time.
hvac-quote-system.solution: Designed a production n8n workflow handling multi-format attachments, custom pricing rules, parts catalog integration, calculation tools, and full conversation history.

proflow-plumbing-voice-ai.title: 24/7 Voice Receptionist for a Plumbing Company
proflow-plumbing-voice-ai.problem: Like most small plumbing businesses, ProFlow was losing real revenue every time the owner couldn't pick up — on a job, on another call, after hours. Every missed call was a customer calling the next plumber on Google.
proflow-plumbing-voice-ai.solution: Built a Retell voice AI receptionist with a Twilio failover: the company number rings the owner's phone for 15 seconds first; if he doesn't pick up, the call routes to the AI. The agent answers business questions from a knowledge base (pricing, service area, hours), and for bookings it calls an n8n workflow that checks the calendar in real time. If the requested slot is free it books and confirms; if not, it offers the 3 closest available times. On confirmation, n8n creates the calendar event, emails the owner, and updates the CRM.
results: Owner never loses a lead to an unanswered phone again
Real-time availability check + booking in a single call
Auto email-to-owner + CRM update on every booking

lead-intake-followup-engine.title: Lead Intake & Personalized Follow-up Engine
lead-intake-followup-engine.problem: Leads were coming in from websites and forms but slipping through the cracks. The owner didn't have time to write personal follow-ups, duplicates were polluting the CRM, and prospects were going cold while waiting for a response.
lead-intake-followup-engine.solution: Built a 3-workflow n8n system. Workflow 1 (Intake): captures leads from any site or form, dedupes against the CRM, then an AI agent writes a 2-sentence personalized profile (name, company, interest, notes), pushes the row into a Google Sheet CRM, emails the owner, and confirms back to the prospect. Workflow 2 (Follow-up Engine): runs a 4-step cadence per lead — greeting on day 0, then day 1, day 3, and day 8 — each message AI-written so it reads like the owner typed it. If the lead replies at any step, follow-ups auto-pause until the owner re-enables them. After 4 unanswered touches, the sequence stops cleanly. Workflow 3 (Owner Actions): lets the owner manually trigger or reschedule a workflow on demand for any lead — full control without waiting for the next cron.
results: Zero leads lost to duplicates or slow response time
4 personalized touches per lead, fully unattended
Auto-pause on reply + manual override give the owner a real CRM, not a spreadsheet

claude-executive-assistant.title: Claude-Powered Social Media Executive Assistant
claude-executive-assistant.problem: A busy founder needed to stay active on two Facebook pages, Instagram, and LinkedIn — but content research, post design, and scheduling were eating hours every week. Most AI tools spit out generic copy; nothing handled the full loop from idea to published post.
claude-executive-assistant.solution: Built a Claude project that runs as a real executive assistant. The owner gives it a topic — or asks it to research what's currently trending in a niche (AI, video editing, anything) — and Claude returns a curated short-list to choose from. Once a topic is picked, it does a second deep-research pass, presents the angle, and asks how to format it: carousel or text + image. It generates the full post (visuals included), and on approval auto-publishes to both Facebook pages, Instagram, and LinkedIn. Same project also drafts YouTube video plans and generates banners.
results: Two-step research loop produces post-ready ideas without generic AI fluff
Carousels, image posts, and YouTube banners generated in-flow
One approval → auto-publishes to 4 social channels at once

faqs.heading: Common Questions
faqs.subheading: The things business owners actually ask before we start working together. If yours isn't here, message my AI on the chat or book a free call.

items.q: What kind of automations do you actually build?
items.a: Voice AI receptionists (especially for service businesses like plumbing, HVAC, dental, salons — so you never miss a call again). CRM automation including Go High Level and similar systems. Lead follow-up sequences, table and meeting booking automation, social media posting, Gmail and Outlook automation, WhatsApp bots, website chatbots, and end-to-end workflows that connect all your tools together. If you do something repetitive every day, it can probably be automated.

items.q: How much does it cost — and how do payments work?
items.a: Price depends on the project; you'll get a clear number on a free 30-minute call. Payment is split 50 / 50: you pay 50% when we start, the remaining 50% when the system is delivered and working. No surprise invoices, no hidden fees. For ongoing managed systems (see below) there's a one-time setup fee plus a small monthly amount.

Three options — pick whichever feels right.

1) Fully managed — I host everything, monitor it, fix anything that breaks. You pay a setup fee + a monthly amount and just use the benefit. Best if you don't want to think about the tech at all.

2) Build and hand over — I build the system, deliver it, and you handle hosting and maintenance going forward. Best if you have an in-house IT team or want full ownership.

3) Build, integrate, and 14-day care — I build it, integrate it into your side, and watch it for 14 days after delivery. Anything that breaks in those 14 days I fix free. After that, you own it. Best for businesses that want a clean handoff with a safety net.

items.q: How long does a project take?
items.a: It depends on the project — a real estimate needs a quick call. As a guide: a normal automation is usually live in around 3 days. A complex one (multi-step workflow, multiple integrations, voice agent with custom logic) is typically 7–10 days. Most things are done in under two weeks. If your project is bigger, I'll tell you straight on the discovery call instead of guessing here.

items.q: Is my data and my customers' data safe?
items.a: Privacy is the most important part. Your data stays in your accounts and your tools — I don't keep copies. For voice and chat, I configure providers so call recordings and transcripts can be deleted on a schedule you choose. Sensitive details (customer info, payment data, internal docs) never go into a public AI without your explicit decision. Anything that touches a specific customer workflow we discuss directly on a call so the setup matches your privacy needs exactly.

items.q: Do you build with no-code tools or real code?
items.a: Both, depending on what fits. Simple to medium automations are built fast and cleanly with n8n, Make, Cal.com, Retell, or Vapi — same tools used by big production teams, easy to extend later. Complex or business-critical systems I build as real code (Claude-Code engineered TypeScript/Node), so they're fast, reliable, and not locked to any platform. You get the right tool for the job, not a one-size-fits-all answer.

items.q: I run a service business and miss calls — can a voice AI really replace that?
items.a: Yes — this is one of my core builds. The voice agent answers 24/7, sounds natural, captures the customer's name, address, and what they need, and either books the job into your calendar or texts you the lead immediately. For plumbing, HVAC, electrical, dental, and similar businesses, every missed call is lost revenue — this is usually the highest-ROI automation I ship.

Book a free discovery call from the button below — about 30 minutes. We talk through what's slowing your business down, I tell you honestly whether automation will help and what it will cost, and you decide. No pitch deck, no pressure.
